title: Student Dictionary
description: Create a dictionary called **student** with keys `'name'` (value `'Alice'`), `'age'` (value `22`), and `'grade'` (value `'A'`). Then use a **for loop with .items()** to print each key and value on its own line in the format: `name: Alice`
starter_code: # Create your dict and loop here

code_requirements.description: Creates a variable called 'student' with a dictionary containing a 'name' key
code_requirements.fail_message: Create a variable called 'student' and assign it a dictionary with a 'name' key.

code_requirements.description: Uses .items() to iterate over key-value pairs
code_requirements.fail_message: Use the .items() method inside your for loop to get both the key and value from the dictionary.

hints: Loop with .items(): for key, value in student.items():
Inside the loop, print with: print(key + ': ' + str(value))
